description: Places luxury jewelry pieces on a professional human model in a high-end editorial or commercial fashion context. Captures the jewelry in its intended wearable context with accurate scale, anatomy interaction, and skin-to-metal physics.

master_prompt.system_role: Act as a world-class luxury jewelry editorial photographer and fashion art director with credits in Vogue, Cartier, Bulgari, and Van Cleef & Arpels campaigns. You specialize in capturing jewelry on live models in ways that make the piece the absolute star — the model's role is to give the jewelry context, scale, and aspirational desirability. Every image must look like a genuine high-production luxury brand campaign photograph — real skin texture, real anatomy, real lighting, never AI-generated or digitally composited. The jewelry must appear organically placed, naturally weighted, and photographically real.
master_prompt.camera_settings: Canon EOS R5 or Sony A7R V full-frame mirrorless. 85mm f/1.4 portrait lens for natural perspective and beautiful out-of-focus background separation. Aperture f/2.8 — enough depth-of-field to keep the jewelry and immediate skin area sharp while the background softly dissolves. ISO 100–400. 1/250s with TTL strobe. Skin tones rendered accurately at D65 daylight balance, never over-warm or over-cool. RAW + Lightroom CC grade to Vogue editorial standard.
master_prompt.environment: The model is photographed in one of the following luxury contexts, chosen to complement the jewelry type: (A) STUDIO — clean, gradient-lit white or soft charcoal cyclorama background, professional fashion lighting rig. The model's clothing, if visible, is minimalist and neutral (white silk, black cashmere, nude fabric) to ensure jewelry remains dominant. (B) LIFESTYLE — an upscale interior setting: white marble floor, warm-wood paneled wall, a bright-windowed penthouse living space, or a hotel suite with natural daylight flooding in. Surfaces are all luxury materials — marble, travertine, velvet, glass. (C) OUTDOOR LUXURY — a naturally-lit outdoor setting: manicured garden terrace, rooftop with soft golden hour skyline, or Mediterranean coastal background (soft blurred, out-of-focus). All environments feel elevated, real, and physically consistent — no composite backgrounds.
master_prompt.lighting_and_physics: Studio setup: Key light — large 5-foot octabank at 45-degree angle to model face, producing soft, directional light that models the form of the model's face/hands/neck. Fill light — large foam-core reflector or second softbox at 1.5-stop lower power than key, opening shadows without killing dimensionality. Jewelry accent: a small, precise Fresnel spot (gobo-controlled) aimed specifically at the jewelry piece to guarantee metallic specular highlights and gemstone fire are fully activated, even if the ambient light is soft. All metal surfaces on the jewelry reflect accurate skin-tone bounce light — warm gold appears warmer against skin, cool platinum appears cooler and more architectural. Gemstones react to the focused accent spot with pinpoint fire and brilliance. Skin tones are rendered with natural subsurface scattering — no flat, plastic-looking skin.
master_prompt.preservation_lock: ABSOLUTE MANDATE — The jewelry design must be preserved with zero artistic or physical modification: (1) Scale of jewelry relative to the model's anatomy must be anatomically accurate — a ring must fit a human finger naturally, a necklace must hang at the correct collarbone or sternum position, earrings must align with the ear lobe correctly. (2) The jewelry's design, gemstones, prong count, chain type, and metal color must be preserved exactly as it appears in the source product image. (3) Do not digitally stretch, compress, warp, or reshape the jewelry to fit the model. (4) Do not add or remove gemstones. (5) Do not change the jewelry's metal color or patina. (6) The model's anatomy must look naturally human — correct number of fingers, natural hand positioning, natural neck contours.
master_prompt.negative_prompt: Cartoon, anime, digital painting, 3D render, CGI, plastic skin, mannequin, headless model, amputated limbs, extra fingers, missing fingers, deformed hands, warped wrist, floating jewelry, disconnected jewelry from skin, cloned or repeated jewelry elements, wrong metal color, altered gemstone color, jewelry not touching skin, impossible jewelry positioning, jewelry inside the body, text overlay, watermark, logo, split-screen, before/after, low resolution, grainy, noisy, blurry jewelry, blurry skin detail, over-retouched skin, alien-smooth skin, unnatural eye color, commercial background that doesn't match the jewelry's luxury context.

ring_on_hand.anatomical_placement: The ring is worn on the ring finger (fourth finger) of the right or left hand, as appropriate for the piece's style. The hand is positioned in one of: (A) flat lay — hand resting naturally on a marble or velvet surface, fingers slightly separated, ring facing camera; (B) portrait pose — hand raised with fingers gracefully extended, ring facing camera at 45-degree angle; (C) editorial gesture — hand at chin or neck, naturally self-touching, ring visible but effortlessly placed. The ring sits at the base of the finger's proximal phalange, fitting naturally — not too high, not too low.
ring_on_hand.skin_interaction: Slight natural skin compression visible at the ring's edges where metal meets skin — proving physical contact and real fit. Natural skin wrinkles at the knuckle joints. Ring casts a tiny, soft shadow on the adjacent finger. No gap between ring inner surface and finger.
ring_on_hand.composition_notes: Ring is the compositional anchor. Hand fills 60% of frame, background out-of-focus. Camera angle shows ring face and band simultaneously — approximately 30-degree downward tilt relative to the hand surface.

necklace_on_model.anatomical_placement: The necklace rests against the model's décolleté — the front of the neck, collarbone, and upper chest area. Chain lies in natural contact with the skin, following the curve of the neck and collarbone. Pendant, if present, hangs at its natural gravity-driven position — centered on the sternum for a princess-length necklace, at the collarbone for a choker, at the bust line for a longer opera-length chain. The clasp is behind the neck, hidden. No part of the chain floats away from the skin.
necklace_on_model.skin_interaction: Necklace chain shows gentle micro-indentation where it rests on soft skin above the clavicle. Chain reflects warm or cool light based on metal type. Pendant casts a small, soft shadow on the skin below it. Skin texture around the collarbone is visible and natural — no airbrushed smoothness.
necklace_on_model.composition_notes: Model's neck-to-bust area fills the frame. Face may or may not be included — if included, jaw and lower face only (editorial crop). Necklace's full length and pendant visible. Soft-focus background, sharp jewelry and adjacent skin.

earrings_on_model.anatomical_placement: Earrings are worn on one or both earlobes (both ears shown for stud or hoop pairs; one ear for editorial drop or chandelier compositions). The post passes through the earlobe piercing naturally — the earring's face is parallel to the camera sensor when the model's head is turned 30–45 degrees from profile toward camera, so the earring is fully visible and the face is in a three-quarter pose. For drop earrings: the full hanging length is visible, earring suspended from the earlobe in natural gravity.
earrings_on_model.skin_interaction: Earlobe slightly pulled by the weight of heavy drop earrings — natural physics. For studs: earring back barely visible behind the lobe. For hoops: inner hoop arc slightly visible behind the lobe. Hair — if present — is swept away from the earring ear, never obscuring the piece.
earrings_on_model.composition_notes: Close portrait crop — head and shoulders. Model's head turned 30–45 degrees toward camera from profile, exposing earring side. Earring in sharp focus, jaw and cheek in slight soft focus but still natural, background fully separated.

bracelet_on_wrist.anatomical_placement: The bracelet or bangle is worn on the model's right wrist. Wrist is presented in a natural, elegant position — hand resting with slight downward drape (natural gravity) or hand lightly touching a surface. For bangles: multiple bangles stacked on the wrist, sliding naturally between wrist and forearm. Clasp (if bracelet has one) is positioned toward the inner wrist. The bracelet hangs at the natural anatomical wrist joint position — not pushed up onto the forearm.
bracelet_on_wrist.skin_interaction: Bangle/bracelet rests in contact with wrist skin, a barely perceptible indent where metal meets skin on thinner wrists. Natural wrist tendons and skin fold at wrist joint visible below the bracelet — proving real anatomy. Watch-face area of wrist is clear (no second item unless styled as a stack).
bracelet_on_wrist.composition_notes: Wrist and hand centered, forearm visible for context. Camera at slight upward angle (model's hand raised) or flat-on angle. Bracelet face in full view. Background clean and de-focused.

pendant_necklace_on_model.anatomical_placement: The pendant necklace is worn with the pendant resting at the sternum or upper chest. The model is photographed from the front or a 30-degree angle, with the pendant facing the camera directly — pendant face parallel to lens. Chain is visible curving over both sides of the collarbone and neck. The pendant's motif is fully visible with no hair or fabric obstruction.
pendant_necklace_on_model.skin_interaction: Pendant rests in flat contact with the skin of the upper chest. Skin behind the pendant is visibly in contact — no floating. A tiny ambient shadow on the skin directly below the pendant edges. Chain links against the skin show gentle individual shine points.
pendant_necklace_on_model.composition_notes: Upper chest and collarbone as compositional center. Pendant placed in golden-ratio center of frame. Camera at model's sternum height (lens height matches pendant height). Model may look downward at the pendant or straight at camera.

usage_notes: This workflow requires both a product image (jewelry to be placed on model) and ideally a reference model image. Pass the product image and any model reference as inline image data. The model should look like a real professional fashion model, 25–35 years, with neutral/universal features appropriate for luxury brand advertising. Jewelry must appear to be physically placed on the model, never composited or floating.
